title: 💸 Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana: How to Open Account in Post Office 2026 🤑
metaTitle: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana: A Comprehensive Guide to Opening an Account in 2026
metaDescription: Learn how to open a Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana account in a post office in 2026. Get all the details on eligibility, documents required, and benefits.

intro.heading: 📈 Introduction to Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana
intro.content: The Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY) is a popular savings scheme launched by the Government of India in 2015. The scheme is designed to secure the financial future of girls in India. 🏦 With a high interest rate and tax benefits, SSY has become a preferred choice for parents looking to save for their daughter's education and marriage expenses. In this article, we will guide you through the process of opening a Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana account in a post office in 2026.

sections.heading: 💸 Benefits of Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana
sections.content: The Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana offers several benefits, including a high interest rate of 7.6% per annum, which is compounded annually. The scheme also provides tax benefits under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act. Additionally, the interest earned and the maturity amount are exempt from tax. 📊 The scheme has a minimum deposit requirement of ₹250 and a maximum deposit limit of ₹1.5 lakhs per annum. The account can be opened for a girl child until she attains the age of 10 years.

sections.heading: 👧 Eligibility Criteria
sections.content: To open a Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana account, the following eligibility criteria must be met: the account can be opened by a parent or guardian for a girl child; the girl child must be a resident of India; the girl child must be below the age of 10 years. ✅ The account can be opened in the name of the girl child, and the parent or guardian will be the guardian of the account until the girl child attains the age of 18 years.

sections.heading: 📝 Documents Required
sections.content: To open a Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana account, the following documents are required: birth certificate of the girl child; identity proof of the parent or guardian (such as PAN card, Aadhaar card, or passport); address proof of the parent or guardian (such as ration card, electricity bill, or passport); a recent photograph of the girl child. 📸 The documents must be self-attested and submitted along with the account opening form.

sections.heading: 🏦 How to Open an Account
sections.content: To open a Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana account in a post office, follow these steps: visit a nearby post office and collect the account opening form; fill the form carefully and submit it along with the required documents; deposit the initial amount of ₹250 or more; the post office will verify the documents and open the account; the account holder will receive a passbook, which will be updated regularly. 📚 The account can also be opened in designated banks, such as State Bank of India, Axis Bank, and ICICI Bank.

faqs.q: What is the minimum deposit requirement for Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana?
faqs.a: The minimum deposit requirement for Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana is ₹250.

faqs.q: What is the maximum deposit limit for Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana?
faqs.a: The maximum deposit limit for Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana is ₹1.5 lakhs per annum.

faqs.q: What is the interest rate offered by Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana?
faqs.a: The interest rate offered by Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana is 7.6% per annum, compounded annually.
